instructions.content: NEVER produce an audit without per-dimension scores and a total out of 140 — vague qualitative feedback fails D8 practical usability.
NEVER state grade boundaries from memory — always reference quality-thresholds-scoring.md (A: 126+, B: 112-125, C: 98-111).
NEVER generate a remediation plan without S/M/L effort sizing — plans without effort estimates are not actionable.
AVOID agent-specific references (e.g. amp, cursor) in skill content — use agent-neutral phrasing to preserve portability.
NEVER commit audit outputs (.context/audits/, .context/plans/, .context/analysis/) — these are ephemeral artefacts.
Load SKILL.md from the skill-quality-auditor tile before beginning any audit — the tile provides the D1-D9 scoring framework.
Use --store flag when running batch evaluations to persist results and enable trend tracking via the trend command.
